title: Exploration of group memberships
text:
The objective is to explore the relation among members of the FRB-MTE-OFB projects. The raw data is not available on Github for privacy issue.

Group membership

devtools::load_all()
library(igraph)
library(visNetwork)

# load reference list
out_data <- here::here("data", "derived-data")
mem <- read.csv(file.path(out_data, "mte_members.csv"))

table(mem$group, mem$function_in_group)

             
              Associate Member PI Post-doc
  Acoucene            0     10  2        1
  Beyonds             0     10  2        1
  Carapat             1      4  2        0
  Comepi              0      8  1        1
  Desybel             1      8  1        0
  Discar              0     10  2        1
  Dragon              0      9  2        1
  Ebenn               0      5  1        1
  Elan                0      6  1        0
  Fellow              0     10  2        1
  Fragshifts          0      8  2        1
  Funbiodiv           0     10  2        1
  Funindic            0      5  1        1
  Impacts             0     10  2        1
  Indicators          0     10  2        1
  Interface           0      5  1        0
  Landbio             0      8  1        0
  Landworm            0      7  3        1
  Motiver             0     10  2        1
  Pppirec             1      1  2        0
  Rodexpo             1      6  2        0
  Solaire-pb          0      7  1        1
  Spatman             0      8  2        1
  Tres-pratic         0      8  1        1

summary(as.numeric(table(mem$group)))

   Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
   4.00    8.50   10.50   10.17   13.00   13.00 

nrow(mem) # 244 membership

[1] 244



# dup <- duplicated(mem[, c("lastname", "firstname")])
# sum(duplicated(mem[, c("lastname", "firstname")])) # 21 ==
# sum(duplicated(mem$emails[!is.na(mem$emails)])) # 21

mem$name <- paste(mem$lastname, mem$firstname)
length(unique(mem$name)) # 223 members

[1] 223




Group network
Based on shared members

group_mem <- table(mem$group, mem$name)
member_overlap <- as.matrix(
  proxy::simil(group_mem, method = "russel", diag = TRUE, upper = TRUE) *
    ncol(group_mem)
)
diag(member_overlap) <- 0

netMEM <- igraph::graph_from_adjacency_matrix(
  member_overlap,
  mode = "undirected",
  weighted = TRUE
)
# plot(
#   netMEM,
#   layout = layout_with_mds(netMEM),
#   rescale = FALSE,
#   edge.width = E(netMEM)$weight
# )
# visNetwork::visIgraph(netMEM, randomSeed = 18)

vcount(netMEM) # 24 nodes

[1] 24

ecount(netMEM) # 19 edges

[1] 19

# very low connectance: 0.06
edge_density(netMEM, loops = FALSE)

[1] 0.06884058

# as a matrix
# netmatrix <- as_adjacency_matrix(netMEM, sparse = F)
# heatmap(netmatrix, scale = "none")

modMEM <- cluster_walktrap(netMEM)
modularity(modMEM)

[1] 0.6899811

modcol <- rainbow(max(membership(modMEM)))[membership(modMEM)]
V(netMEM)$color <- modcol
visNetwork::visIgraph(netMEM, randomSeed = 54)







Member network
Based on group membership

pw <- c()

for (i in sort(unique(mem$group))) {
  ni <- mem$name[mem$group == i]
  pwi <- expand.grid(ni, ni)
  #remove identity / loop
  pwi <- pwi[pwi$Var1 != pwi$Var2, ]
  pw <- rbind(pw, pwi)
}

netRES <- igraph::graph_from_data_frame(pw, directed = FALSE)

# plot(
#   netRES,
#   layout = layout_with_mds(netRES),
#   vertex.label = NA
# )

vcount(netRES) # 223 nodes : members

[1] 223

ecount(netRES) # 2406 connections

[1] 2406

# low connectance: 0.09
edge_density(netRES, loops = FALSE)

[1] 0.09720034

# netmatrix <- as_adjacency_matrix(netRES, sparse = FALSE)
# heatmap(netmatrix, scale = "none")

wtc <- cluster_walktrap(netRES)
modularity(wtc)

[1] 0.8578844

modcol <- rainbow(max(membership(wtc)))[membership(wtc)]
V(netRES)$color <- modcol
visNetwork::visIgraph(netRES)

title: Exploration of CESAB groups membership
text:
The objective is to explore the relation among members of the FRB CESAB. The raw data is not available on Github for privacy issue.

Group membership

devtools::load_all()
library(igraph)
library(visNetwork)

# load reference list
out_data <- here::here("data", "derived-data")
cesab <- read.csv(file.path(out_data, "cesab_members.csv"))

# remove guest and stagiaire
cesab <- cesab[!cesab$function_in_group %in% c("Guest", "Stagiaire"), ]

# remove non active members (and keep NA)
cesab <- cesab[!cesab$still_member %in% 0, ]

table(cesab$group, cesab$function_in_group)

title: Exploration of bibliometrix R package
text:
The objective is to explore the functionalities of the bibliometrix R package.
The example case study is based on the references cited by projects financed under the FRB-MTE-OFB projects. The references were cleaned and completed in the scripts 01_clean_references.R and 02_fetch_openalex.R.
The bibliometrix package has a nice Shiny interface that can be accessed locally with:
bibliometrix::biblioshiny()
The openalex data in can be loaded in the Shinyapp in the section Data > Import raw files > OpenAlexAPI.
But here the objective is to use and explore the functions of bibliometrix package.

title: Exploration of bibliometrix R package
text:
Data loading and converting
The data loading takes few minutes to run.

library(bibliometrix)
oadata <- here::here("data", "derived-data", "mte_references_oa.rdata")

M <- bibliometrix::convert2df(
  file = oadata,
  dbsource = "openalex_api",
  format = "api"
)


Converting your openalex_api collection into a bibliographic dataframe


Done!


Removed  97 duplicated documents



We can check the quality of the bibliometric data.

com <- missingData(M)
print(com$mandatoryTags)

   tag          description missing_counts missing_pct             status

title: Exploration of bibliometrix R package
text:
Simple bibliometric Analysis
A recommended first step is a descriptive analysis of the bibliographic data with the function biblioAnalysis. It calculates:

Annual scientific production
Most productive authors
Most cited manuscripts
Most productive countries
Total citations per country
Most relevant sources (journals)
Most frequent keywords


results <- biblioAnalysis(M, sep = ";")
S <- summary(object = results, k = 10, pause = FALSE)

Local citations
Local citations measure how many times an author (or a document) included in the collection have been cited within the collection.

title: Exploration of bibliometrix R package
text:
Bibliographic network matrices

Co-Citation Network

NetMatrix <- biblioNetwork(
  M,
  analysis = "co-citation",
  network = "references",
  n = 30,
  sep = ";"
)

# Plot the network
net <- networkPlot(
  NetMatrix,
  Title = "Co-Citation Network",
  type = "fruchterman",
  size = T,
  remove.multiple = FALSE,
  labelsize = 0.7,
  edgesize = 5
)










Keyword co-occurrences

# Create keyword co-occurrences network
NetMatrix <- biblioNetwork(
  M,
  analysis = "co-occurrences",
  network = "keywords",
  sep = ";"
)

# Plot the network
net <- networkPlot(
  NetMatrix,
  normalize = "association",
  weighted = T,
  n = 30,
  Title = "Keyword Co-occurrences",
  type = "fruchterman",
  size = T,
  edgesize = 5,
  labelsize = 0.7
)

title: Exploration of bibliometrix R package
text:
Thematic map

Co-Word Analysis

CS <- conceptualStructure(
  M,
  field = "ID",
  method = "MCA",
  minDegree = 10,
  clust = 6,
  stemming = FALSE,
  labelsize = 15,
  documents = 20,
  graph = FALSE
)
plot(CS$graph_terms)







# plot(CS$graph_dendogram)



Thematic map

thematicMapResults <- thematicMap(
  M,
  field = "DE",
  n = 250,
  minfreq = 5,
  stemming = FALSE,
  size = 0.3,
  n.labels = 3,
  repel = TRUE,
  cluster = "louvain"
)
plot(thematicMapResults$map)







plot(thematicMapResults$net$graph)

title: Overview
text:
Overview
Exploration with MTE dataset

Exploration of bibliometrix output

Exploration of group member network

Exploration with CESAB dataset 2. Exploration of group member network
